content.problemStatement:
In C, matrices are represented using 2D arrays, which enable element-wise access for performing operations such as addition, subtraction, and multiplication. These operations are essential in linear algebra and scientific computing.

---

1. Matrix Addition: Add corresponding elements of two matrices of the same dimensions.
```c
result[i][j] = matrix1[i][j] + matrix2[i][j];
```

2. Matrix Subtraction: Subtract elements of one matrix from another at the same positions.
```c
result[i][j] = matrix1[i][j] - matrix2[i][j];
```
3. Matrix Multiplication: Multiply rows of the first matrix by columns of the second matrix and sum the products.

```c
// k iterates over the shared dimension (columns of matrix1 and rows of matrix2)
for (k = 0; k < c1; k++) {
    result[i][j] += matrix1[i][k] * matrix2[k][j]; // += accumulates the sum of products for result[i][j]
}
```

---
**Note:**

Matrix multiplication is allowed only when the number of columns in the first matrix equals the number of rows in the second matrix.
